title: FCRA Rules tightened: NGOs must declare social media accounts, stick to specified activities; political content barred
content: All NGOs and associations registered under FCRA, 2010 are required to disclose specific activities and geographical scope of their programmes

title: Kerala govt plans college incubation centres to foster tech-based MSMEs, says Minister P.K. Kunhalikutty
content: Industries and IT Minister says preliminary discussions have already been with Higher Education Minister to formulate policies aimed at strengthening academic-industry collaboration

title: Bengaluru to host BRICS heads of space agencies meet on June 23-24
content: Heads and senior representatives of the space agencies of the 11 BRICS member countries will attend the meeting

title: Kerala’s domestic power generation meets only 17% of demand; State facing 900 MW deficit: Electricity Minister
content: Sunny Joseph says despite recording substantial growth in electricity generation capacity in the last decade, Kerala continues to rely heavily on power procured from outside State to meet its rising requirements

title: Jaishankar meets Mongolian leaders, reviews bilateral cooperation
content: Jaishankar said the India-Mongolia relationship is rooted in "deep civilisational and spiritual heritage, shared democratic values, strong development aspirations and strong people-to-people ties".

title: Malayali filmmaker Kunjila Mascillamani on entering the TIFF Directors’ Lab for ‘Guptam’ and struggles of female indie filmmakers
content: Guptam was among the 16 films selected for the TIFF Director’s Lab, and is backed by filmmakers Payal Kapadia, Jeo Baby and actor Kani Kusruti

title: Vijay government moves Supreme Court on Thirupparankundram row
content: The High Court’s Division Bench had earlier confirmed a previous Single Judge Bench order directing the Subramaniya Swamy Temple management to light the Karthigai Deepam at the ‘deepathoon’

title: Two workers killed in blast at Dakshin Energy unit in Parawada near Visakhapatnam’s Pharma City
content: Both men were the only workers on the night shift at the Parawada unit, near Pharma City; the Factories department will probe the cause

title: Ayodhya Ram temple donation case: SIT submits preliminary report to U.P. government
content: The Uttar Pradesh government had on June 13 constituted the SIT on a request from the temple trust, following allegations of misappropriation of donations received at the Ayodhya Ram temple

title: Industry leaders hail West Bengal Budget’s push on infrastructure, land reform and health
content: Representatives of CII, ASSOCHAM and MCC welcomed the provisions highlighted in the first budget tabled by the Bharatiya Janata Party government in West Bengal.

title: Plea claims ration linked to SIR in West Bengal, SC asks petitioner to move HC
content: A plea in the Supreme Court claims  the outcome of the SIR has been linked to the working of the PDS and Annapurna Yojana.

title: IMD forecasts light to moderate rain in Hyderabad on June 23
content: Rain in Hyderabad could be accompanied by gusty winds. Roads and low-lying areas at a few locations may be waterlogged.

title: 'Engine of plunder' running at full speed under BJP's double-engine govt in MP: Congress
content: “Under the BJP's 'double-engine' government in Madhya Pradesh, an 'engine of plunder' is running at full speed," said Jairam Ramesh

title: The need for strengthening India’s EV supply chains
content: As EV adoption gathers pace, there is a greater need to focus on reducing vulnerability to external shocks

title: T.N. Chief Minister Vijay’s close aide Jagadish Palanisamy appointed as CM’s Private Secretary
content: Mr Palanisamy, also a film producer, was appointed by the State government on May 12, two days after Mr. Vijay assumed office as Chief Minister

title: George Kurian resigns as Union Minister, likely to return to Kerala politics
content: Union Minister of State for Minority Affairs Mr. Kurian has resigned from the council of ministers after his Rajya Sabha term came to an end

title: Over 3 kg of gold seized from residence of arrested Trinamool leader Sabyasachi Dutta’s aide
content: Sabyasachi Dutta was arrested by the Bidhannagar City Police on June 8 on charges of extortion. Earlier, the investigators have seized about three and a half kg of gold from the former MLAs  flat and bank locker.

title: Over 6 lakh vehicles hit Hyderabad’s IT corridor as overnight rain leaves roads waterlogged, snarls rush-hour traffic
content: Nearly a million vehicles were recorded in Cyberabad Police Commissionerate limits by noon on June 23, 2026, even as the police asked companies to consider skeleton staffing and remote working arrangements

title: Traffic snarls on NEET day: Congress and BJP indulge in war of words
content: While Bengaluru South MP Tejasvi Surya alleged that several students had missed the re-examination, the Congress refuted the claim.

title: NEET candidate delay: Bengaluru Traffic Police rule out event-related congestion
content: Police said CCTV camera footage and route analysis confirmed that the candidate departed from home 33 minutes before the cut-off time, and also chose a longer route

title: 3 students killed as speeding tipper crashes into bus stop in Kerala’s Kottarakara
content: The lorry, which was heavily loaded with sand, overturned completely upon impact; eyewitnesses said that the vehicle was traveling at an excessive speed before the driver lost control.

title: An industrial risk the Qatar blast shares with accidents in India | Explained
content: Industry data consistently show that nearly 50% of all process safety incidents occur during 10% of an industrial plant’s operations, when it is in a transient mode

title: Kerala Assembly: LDF Opposition walks out over low-alcohol liquor tax row
content: LDF brought the issue to Assembly’s attention through Rule 50 notice. But Speaker refused to admit adjournment motion, ruling that the issue did not meet the level of urgency required to suspend House proceedings for detailed discussion

title: Kerala’s first sci-fi adventure play heads to Thrissur
content: Adapted from Ross Welford’s novel, The Kid Who Came From Space, and directed by Vishnu Prasad, the play explores the idea of coexistence through the friendship between humans and an alien

title: Leopard that strayed into Kelakam in Kerala’s Kannur trapped and released into deep forest
content: The leopard, estimated to be a five-year-old male, was found to be in good health after a detailed examination by veterinary doctor

title: Lucknow building fire: Mother's plea, colleague’s distress call capture horror
content: The blaze erupted at the building housing an animation centre on Usha Mehta Marg, leaving 15 people dead and seven injured

title: 15 die in Lucknow building fire, victims mostly students; three building owners arrested
content: Videos show youth falling from the building in Lucknow next to roadside railing; State govt forms SIT to probe incident; PM expresses grief, announces ₹2 lakh for kin of the deceased

title: Disproportionate assets case: Telangana ACB raids properties linked to Nizamabad Excise Superintendent Mallareddy
content: The searches were initiated following allegations that the official possessed assets disproportionate to his known sources of income

title: Devastating building fire in Lucknow brings development authority’s actions under scrutiny
content: The building had been issued a demolition order in 2016 over unauthorised construction, but it was revoked within two months

title: Fire accidents since Independence – A timeline
content: From The Hindu’s archives, a look at some of major fire accidents across the country since Independence

title: PM Modi must stop appeasing 'good friend' Trump; India must not sign trade pact as it stands: Congress
content: There is absolutely no need for India to be bamboozled into signing any trade agreement which as it stands now is heavily against India's interests, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: 42 Umrah pilgrims escape unhurt as bus catches fire near Kannur airport in Kerala
content: Bus from Wayanad caught fire in Shivapuram. After the driver noticed flames at the rear end of the vehicle, he  stopped the bus. The sleeping passengers were soon evacuated safely

title: Hundreds of farmers join ‘Bairamangala Chalo’ rally against Bidadi Township Project
content: The rally marks the first large-scale farmers protest against the project, although local protests are ongoing for over 460 days.

title: 25 industrial projects moved out of Tamil Nadu during DMK government’s rule, alleges Minister
content: She says Tamil Nadu’s youth lost employment opportunities amounting to 2.03 lakh jobs because of this migration

title: Combined water levels in seven lakes that supply water to Mumbai is at 8.3% of useful capacity
content: Water levels in larger lakes where Mumbai gets its supply from are extremely low, and much lower compared to this time last year.

title: Indian children among most exposed to extreme heat, drought, and multiple climate hazards: UNICEF Report
content: Data from the UNICEF’s Children’s Climate Risk Report 2026 shows that vulnerability of Indian children to climate hazards is high, especially to extreme heat.

title: World Music Day: Can music help you relax? India’s wellness labels think so
content: As mental health discussions rise, music’s therapeutic potential is in question again, exploring its calming effects. The increasing popularity of wellness music festivals further underscores a collective yearning for deeper, transformative experiences in today’s fast-paced world

title: Cab driver killed, five Mysuru BEOs injured in Mysuru-Bengaluru highway crash
content: The five BEOs of Mysuru district were on their way to attend a meeting in Bengaluru when their hired vehicle collided with a goods vehicle

title: Uttara Kannada man dies of H1N1 in Mangaluru
content: The man, a farm labourer from Yallapur taluk in Uttara Kannada district, was suffering from fever and had got himself treated at a private clinic.

title: No free diagnostic services for women with more than three children: Assam hospital
content: Assembly Speaker Ranjeet Kumar Dass, who is president of a First Referral Unit in Barpeta district, says he has advised State Health Minister Ashok Singhal to implement this system in all government healthcare facilities across the State

title: 100 years of Mehboob: A legacy that lives on
content: World Music Day: Generations of music lovers reminisce the stories and songs of maverick musician H Mehboob

title: India leads world in ship recycling, captures 35.4% global share
content: The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways said that according to the latest report by the UNCTAD, India increased its share of global ship recycling activity to 35.4% in 2025 from 30.1% in 2024

title: Will launch fast from July 5 if amendments to RTI Rules not revoked: Anna Hazare
content: Mr. Hazare objected to the hike in fees, saying no rational explanation or financial analysis was provided.

title: Rebel Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Omraje Nimbalkar to join Shinde-led Shiv Sena
content: Mr. Nimbalkar arrived in Dharashiv from Pune in the afternoon and interacted with his supporters. He cited the Sena (UBT)'s defeat in the local body elections and his inability to do much for the people because of being in Opposition

title: Massive showdown in TN assembly: CM Vijay targets DMK, mimics Stalin; opposition walks out
content: Tamil Nadu chief minister Vijay engaged in a heated exchange with the opposition DMK in the assembly, accusing them of misusing party funds and engaging in political opportunism. Amidst disruptions, he mimicked a viral hand gesture associated with former CM MK Stalin, leading to a DMK walkout. Vijay defended his party's alliances and addressed concerns on law and order, asserting his government's commitment to tackling corruption and crime.

title: 11-year-old Delhi girl kidnapped, raped and murdered near Gurgaon border; 1 held
content: A horrific incident has gripped Delhi as an 11-year-old girl was allegedly kidnapped, brutally assaulted, and murdered after being taken from a South Delhi footpath. Police swiftly arrested a suspect following a rigorous investigation involving CCTV analysis and technical evidence. The victim's body was discovered in a forested area near the Delhi-Gurugram border. Authorities are continuing their interrogation to uncover any potential accomplices.

title: Amid rebellion, TMC sends fresh office-bearers list to EC, says Mamata remains party chief
content: Trinamool Congress has reshuffled its national working committee, asserting Mamata Banerjee's leadership following a significant electoral setback. This move aims to counter a rebellion by a faction claiming to be the "real" TMC, which appointed its own chairperson. The party has informed the Election Commission of its new office-bearers, with Banerjee remaining chairperson and her nephew Abhishek Banerjee continuing as national general secretary.

title: 'Felt like a dream': Sooryavanshi gets emotional after getting India jersey - Watch
content: At 15 years and 71 days, Sooryavanshi has become the youngest player selected in India's senior men's squad, surpassing the record set by Sachin Tendulkar 36 years ago.

title: 'India welcomes US-Iran MoU': NSA Ajit Doval expresses 'cautious optimism' over peace deal
content: India's National Security Advisor Ajit Doval expressed 'cautious optimism' regarding the US-Iran MoU, hoping it restores regional stability and strengthens energy security. He highlighted the potential for easing global supply chain disruptions and improving economic prosperity through the Strait of Hormuz. Doval also addressed the world's complex security landscape and the weakening of multilateral institutions, emphasizing BRICS' role in fostering a multipolar order and cooperation among emerging economies.

title: Meta to US employees: We’re installing tracking software; later confirms private data leak
content: Meta has paused its controversial Model Capability Initiative (MCI), the AI tracking program that logged US employees' keystrokes, mouse clicks, and screen activity, after an internal leak exposed sensitive data across 45,000 tables companywide. The breach laid bare private conversations, transcriptions, and performance data, confirming the exact security risk over 1,600 workers had warned about in a petition. Meta says it is now investigating the incident.

title: Ayodhya donation scam probe: SIT submits preliminary report to UP govt
content: A Special Investigation Team has submitted its initial findings to the Uttar Pradesh government regarding allegations of embezzlement of donations for the Ayodhya Ram temple. The probe, initiated by the temple trust, is actively continuing as investigators gather more information. Officials confirmed the preliminary report was handed over to the Additional Chief Secretary (Home) by the Lucknow Divisional Commissioner, with further details expected as the investigation progresses.

title: 'France will probably beat us': Haaland's reality check ahead of Mbappe showdown
content: The Manchester City forward now has four goals in the World Cup, level with France's Kylian Mbappe and one behind Lionel Messi. Norway's victory also takes them to six points in Group I, alongside France, with the two sides set to meet on Friday to decide the group winner.

title: 'Bring a diaper, write your demand': CJP's new protest plan at 6pm today
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) continues its four-day protest at Jantar Mantar, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-UG paper leak. Organizers reported police attempts to restrict protest space overnight. The CJP has announced a "diaper donation drive" and dedicated a day for UPSC and SSC aspirants to voice concerns, vowing to continue their indefinite agitation.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Bhu-Aadhaar for land: How a 14-digit ID can help property buyers to avoid disputes
content: India is introducing Bhu-Aadhaar, a 14-digit Unique Land Parcel Identification Number (ULPIN), to digitally identify every land parcel. This initiative aims to simplify property verification by creating a standard digital record, making transactions easier for buyers, banks, and government agencies. Based on geo-referenced coordinates, ULPIN ensures a unique digital identity tied to each plot's exact location, strengthening land administration and potentially reducing disputes.

title: ITR filing FY 2025-26: How to calculate taxes under old income tax regime - explained
content: For many people the old income tax regime continues to be attractive despite the new regime offering higher basic exemption and standard deduction, rebate limits and lower tax rates.

title: Will Ronaldo be benched vs Uzbekistan? Portugal coach drops hints before match day
content: Portugal's coach Roberto Martínez remained tight-lipped about Cristiano Ronaldo's starting position for the upcoming Uzbekistan match, following a subdued performance against Congo. Despite external criticism, Martínez defended his squad, emphasising unity and dismissing 'unfair' commentary. He highlighted Ronaldo's continued value in creating space and scoring opportunities.

title: Germany sends a clear message to America: NASA can’t operate without us
content: Germany highlights Europe's crucial role in US space endeavors, stating missions like lunar flights are impossible without European technology. This assertion comes amid broader EU efforts to achieve "tech sovereignty" and reduce reliance on American giants in cloud, AI, and microchips. Concerns over US tech access, amplified by recent AI model bans, are driving Europe to develop its own alternatives and bolster domestic capacity, aiming to reclaim global geoeconomic influence.

title: 'Called to shoot reels, then raped': Delhi influencer alleges sexual assault in Burari hotel
content: A social media influencer has accused a man of rape, alleging he lured her to a hotel for video shoots and then sexually assaulted her. The incident, which occurred in September 2022, was followed by alleged blackmail and repeated assaults. The victim also reported threats from the accused's relatives after she ended contact. Police have registered an FIR and are investigating.

title: 'He hurt himself very, very badly': Trump's blunt response to Starmer's resignation as UK PM
content: US President Donald Trump has sharply criticized outgoing UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's policies on energy, immigration, and foreign relations, suggesting they contributed to his downfall. Trump specifically pointed to Starmer's approach to North Sea oil and the UK's energy independence. He also mentioned a disagreement over the use of a British military base in Cyprus for US strikes on Iran, which he believes "hurt him badly."

title: After Pakistan, now Bangladesh eyes China’s J-10CE fighter: Why it matters
content: Bangladesh is reportedly set to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets, a significant move for both nations. This deal, if finalized, would mark the second export for China's advanced fighter, following Pakistan's acquisition. The procurement aims to bolster the Bangladeshi Air Force's combat capabilities, replacing aging F-7 aircraft. This development occurs as China's defense exports face a downturn, with Asia being a primary market.

title: Tom Brady sends clear message after Gisele’s alleged Father’s Day snub
content: Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bundchen, has continued to dominate headlines even after years of their divorce. The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bundchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.

title: New Champions crowned, shocking betrayal headlines explosive WWE Raw Night
content: London's O2 Arena witnessed a dramatic WWE RAW go-home show for Night of Champions.  A beloved star turned heel, shocking fans, while Chad Gable faced his former stablemates.  Key bouts saw LA Knight triumph over Jimmy Uso amidst Bloodline interference, and The Street Profits capture the WWE World Tag Team Titles in a chaotic main event, culminating in a surprise championship coronation.

title: Who is Geno Smith’s alleged Ex? Inside the claims stirring NFL attention
content: The New York Jets’ star player, Geno Smith, was gearing up for the next season when he found himself in major legal trouble after an alleged ex girlfriend accused him of domestic violence and being neglectful of a child just a few hours ago. The controversial accusations have sparked shockwaves across the league and many are curious about Geno Smith’s alleged ex girlfriend.

title: FIFA WC: Norway's Viking Row shakes stands after knockout qualification - Watch
content: The Viking Row has become a popular tradition among Norway fans during the tournament. Supporters sit in a formation resembling a Viking longboat and mimic rowing movements to the beat of a drum, drawing comparisons with Iceland's famous "Viking Clap" from Euro 2016.

title: SEBI's new ease-of-business push: What may change for stock exchanges, brokers
content: SEBI is embarking on a substantial reform of stock market regulations designed to simplify operational procedures and alleviate compliance burdens. This review focuses on consolidating rules, minimizing redundancies, and reallocating responsibilities among market infrastructure institutions. Major proposals encompass the creation of a unified master circular for exchanges, updated technology frameworks, and refined reporting processes, ultimately aiming to improve the ease of doing business for all market participants.

title: India taps US for cooking gas: LPG imports to cross 1 M tonnes amid Middle East disruption
content: India's LPG imports from the US are set to hit a record 1 million tonnes in June, a significant shift driven by Middle Eastern supply disruptions. Despite higher costs, New Delhi is prioritizing uninterrupted cooking fuel for households. While traditional suppliers are gradually recovering, the US has become a crucial alternative source, highlighting a strategic pivot in energy sourcing.

title: Assam HSLC Compartment Result 2026 declared at sebaonline.org: Direct link, steps to download marksheet
content: The Assam State School Education Board (ASSEB) has declared the HSLC Compartment Result 2026 on June 23 at 11 AM. Students who appeared for the supplementary examination can access and download their scorecards from the official website, sebaonline.org, using their roll number and login credentials. Candidates who qualify will be eligible for Class 11 admissions and other academic opportunities. The board has also advised students to verify details on their marksheets and follow official updates.

title: 'It was madness': Boycott slams Root's captaincy, thanks 'heavens' for Stokes' return
content: Geoffrey Boycott has slammed Joe Root's captaincy in England's second Test vs New Zealand, saying he's learned nothing  from his previous spell in charge. Boycott welcomed Ben Stokes' return for the next Test. He praised Root's batting but questioned his tactical decisions, particularly his handling of Jofra Archer's bowling workload, drawing parallels to past mistakes.

title: 'Goal of overtaking the world': Kim calls for major expansion of North Korea's nukes
content: North Korea's Kim Jong-un has declared a significant expansion of the nation's nuclear arsenal, aiming to "overtake the world" in defense capabilities. During a key party meeting, officials reaffirmed their commitment to nuclear forces as crucial for national sovereignty and deterring war. The agenda also included accelerating naval projects, reinforcing border defenses, and addressing economic development, particularly in the coal sector. Leadership changes were also announced without explanation.

title: Gold price prediction: Why is gold not rising too much despite lower oil prices?
content: Gold price prediction today: A consistent rise in gold prices due to drop in oil prices is being constrained by hawkish outlook from central banks which are signalling a possible rate hike to keep inflationary pressures in check, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: Jharkhand high court raises divorced woman's alimony from Rs 10 lakh to Rs 40 lakh
content: The Jharkhand High Court significantly increased a divorced woman's alimony from Rs 10 lakh to Rs 40 lakh. The court recognized her ongoing medical needs and the impact of inflation, ensuring she can maintain a dignified life. This decision acknowledges the husband's financial capacity, including his railway retirement benefits, and upholds the principle of providing support post-divorce.

title: With spears & swords, Nihangs stay locked inside Uttarakhand gurdwara
content: A tense standoff at a Uttarakhand gurdwara entered its third day with Nihang Sikhs holding out, reportedly demanding the release of four arrested individuals. Punjab's Chief Minister has urged an amicable resolution through talks. While police maintain the incident is unrelated to a prior clash in Karnaprayag, a Sikh organization suggests the dispute stemmed from accommodation issues for pilgrims heading to Hemkund Sahib.

title: 'I've zero goals in two World Cups': Zlatan bows to Messi's record-breaking march
content: After Messi scored twice in Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria on Monday to become the highest goalscorer in FIFA World Cup history, Ibrahimovic reflected on the 38-year-old's achievement.

title: Illegal Lucknow building had 2016 demolition order, someone revoked it within 60 days
content: A devastating fire has cast a shadow over a Lucknow building's past. Documents reveal a 2016 demolition order for unauthorized construction was controversially revoked within two months. The property, initially for residential use, was later allegedly used commercially, a key focus in the ongoing fire investigation. Officials are probing the circumstances surrounding the order's reversal.

title: Nitish Reddy ruled out of T20Is against Ireland; unlikely for England series too
content: It is learnt that Reddy's quadriceps injury, which kept him out of the 2nd ODI against Afghanistan in Lucknow, has flared up and requires attention before he can take the field again. He will miss the two-match T20I series against Ireland and is a major doubt for the white-ball contests against England that follow.

title: Telangana school bandh today: Schools likely to remain closed as ABVP protests fee hikes and reforms
content: ABVP has called for a statewide school bandh in Telangana over concerns related to private school fee hikes, government school infrastructure gaps, vacant teaching posts and delayed uniform distribution. Several schools in Hyderabad announced holidays ahead of the protest. The organisation has demanded fee regulation laws, stronger monitoring of educational institutions, implementation of NEP-2020 and improvements in government school facilities. Parents have raised concerns about disruptions to classes during the academic year.

title: FIFA World Cup Golden Boot race: Messi, Mbappé and Haaland ignite scoring battle
content: The FIFA World Cup Golden Boot race is heating up as Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé, and Erling Haaland dominate with impressive goal tallies. Messi and Mbappé netted braces for their respective nations, while Haaland also bagged two goals for Norway. Messi has now become the World Cup's all-time leading scorer, leading the Golden Boot standings with five goals.

title: 'Foundation for a final deal': US hits pause on Iranian oil sanctions after nuclear push
content: The US has temporarily lifted sanctions on Iranian oil for 60 days, allowing its production and sale. This move follows Iran's agreement to permit UN nuclear inspectors and ensure free passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Vice President JD Vance hailed the understanding as a crucial foundation for a future deal, though nuclear talks are still in early stages. Any potential asset releases would be restricted for American benefit.

title: NRI couple’s American dream turns into Rs 8.4 crore debt trap
content: An NRI couple's American Dream has spiraled into an ₹8.4 crore debt trap, accumulated through seemingly normal life upgrades like a primary home, investment property, and renovations.  Living on "autopilot," they now face a staggering $850,000 debt.  Their viral story highlights the silent accumulation of financial liabilities and their ambitious 1,200-day plan to achieve debt freedom, prompting a wider conversation on mindful spending.

title: Drag races, drinking parties: Fatal BMW crash exposes misuse of Mumbai-Delhi Expressway
content: A tragic BMW crash on an under-construction expressway near Badlapur, claiming two young lives, highlights the rampant illegal use of the stretch for drag racing and parties. Locals report frequent high-speed incidents, with police investigating the fatal accident. Authorities are urged to secure the unfinished highway to prevent further tragedies and illegal activities.

title: Hardik Pandya joins CoE’s new performance block programme for comeback
content: New initiative, integrated with return to play plan, started for contracted players to work on specific cricket skills round the year.

title: Petrol bomb hurled at BJP leader's husband clinic in Punjab
content: Unidentified assailants on a motorbike hurled a petrol bomb at Dr. Tarsem Garg's clinic in Bathinda late Monday night. The fiery projectile landed in the courtyard, causing minor damage to a parked scooter but no injuries. Dr. Garg, whose wife is a BJP nominee and whose clinic is near the RSS district office, expressed bewilderment regarding the motive. Authorities have launched an investigation, forming teams to apprehend the culprits.

title: Keeping shopping bags and boxes doesn’t mean you’re a hoarder, psychology says
content: Many people save everyday items like plastic bags and boxes, often feeling sheepish. However, psychologists distinguish this from clinical hoarding disorder, which stems from a deep, debilitating attachment to possessions.  While some save items out of a 'preparedness' mindset, true hoarders struggle to separate their identity from their belongings, experiencing distress when discarding them. The research suggests the average saver has less attachment than assumed.

title: Oil prices rebound as US-Iran peace hopes temper Hormuz supply fears
content: On Tuesday, oil prices enjoyed a modest rise as they bounced back from a previous decline. Investors are tentatively hopeful about the ongoing US-Iran peace discussions, yet they remain cautious about the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz for crude exports. Despite early indicators showing improved maritime movements and declining US strategic oil reserves, skepticism lingers as traders look for solid proof of restoring shipping operations.

title: 2 charred to death, 2 trapped as massive fire erupts at Andhra energy plant
content: Tragedy struck Dakshin Energy's Paravada facility Tuesday morning as a massive fire claimed the lives of two young workers, Trinadh (27) and Venkatesh (24). Two others are feared trapped amidst the inferno, which is believed to have been fueled by the large plastic inventory. Firefighting efforts are ongoing, with investigations into the cause set to commence once the blaze is contained.

title: 'Not spending time thinking about my age': Messi after rewriting World Cup history
content: Lionel Messi shattered FIFA World Cup records, becoming the all-time leading goalscorer and most victorious player just before his 39th birthday. Despite the milestones, Messi emphasised his focus remains on fitness and feeling good, not his age. He expressed uncertainty about playing in Argentina's next match but praised the team's competitive spirit, even after a missed penalty initially disrupted their rhythm.

title: "Don't Use Our Roads If...": Madhya Pradesh Minister's Comment Sparks Row
content: The statement drew sharp criticism from the Congress, which accused the senior BJP leader of using divisive language and undermining constitutional values.

title: Lakhs Of Devotees Turn Up For Ambubachi Mela At Kamakhya Temple
content: According to Kamakhya authorities, more than 8 lakh devotees and sadhus from different parts of the country are expected to participate this year.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Trade Flat Amid Mixed Global Cues, Crude Over $78
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude gained 0.38% to $78.15 a barrel and US WTI rose to $74.19 a barrel.

title: UK's Keir Starmer Leaves Without Managing To Explain What He Stood For
content: In opposition, Starmer's stolid managerialism helped Labour influence a nation tired of the shenanigans of Boris Johnson.

title: US Lifts Iran Oil Sanctions: Why India Could Be The Big Winner
content: The US Treasury has issued a temporary general licence allowing transactions involving Iranian crude oil, petroleum products and petrochemicals.

title: 2 Students Beat Bengaluru Landlord To Death With Cricket Bat Over Water Use
content: The victim, identified as Madhav Matle, owned the Laxmi Narasimha PG. He was allegedly assaulted by two college students after an argument escalated into violence.

title: Ransom Note Reveals US Anchor's Mother Died Shortly After Disappearance
content: CNN reported Monday that one of notes revealed that Nancy Guthrie was dead - and those who kidnapped her did not mean to kill her, but she died shortly after her disappearance.

title: JD Vance Says Iran Talks Lay 'Good Foundation' For Deal To End War
content: JD Vance and U.S. officials claimed progress on multiple fronts, including the establishment of "mechanisms" to ensure the Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway for global energy shipments, stays open

title: How Ukraine's Latest Drone Strategy Is Targeting Russia's Supply Lines
content: The campaign relies on a new generation of Ukrainian-made mid-range drones, including models such as the FP-2 and the Behemoth. These drones can travel between 50 and 300 kilometres

title: Petrol Bomb Thrown At BJP Leader's Clinic In Punjab's Bathinda
content: The incident has sparked fear among residents and raised serious questions about the preparedness of the police, especially in light of the Chief Minister's imminent visit.

title: Years Of Paying Rent? How Rental History Can Unlock Your First Home
content: India's credit ecosystem has expanded over the past decade, yet a large section of the population remains outside the formal lending framework.

title: SpaceX Had A Blockbuster Listing. Then It Lost $600 Billion In Just 3 Days
content: Retail trading in SpaceX, officially named Space Exploration Technologies Corp., was the strongest of any IPO in recent history.

title: "Papa, There's Fire, Save Me": Lucknow Man's Last Call To Father For Help
content: A total of 24 people were taken to hospital, of whom 15 were declared dead. Witnesses said the fire started on the ground floor in shops dealing with animals and gradually spread through the building.

title: 'The Timeline Is Months': Five Eyes' Big Warning Over Next AI Breakthrough
content: The warning came in a joint statement from the Five Eyes Intelligence Oversight and Review Council (FIORC), a grouping of intelligence and security entities from Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United Kingdom and the United States.

title: "He's Got 2 Problems": Trump Criticism Follows Starmer's Exit
content: US President Donald Trump said Keir Starmer was "sort of a friend of mine" but had not been supportive enough of the United States on NATO and the Iran war.
